title: 10 best herbal capsules for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for numbness in hands are a natural remedy that helps alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands due to poor circulation, nerve damage, or other underlying conditions. The best herbal capsules for numbness in hands include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Capsicum annuum, Angelica sinensis, Panax ginseng, Piper nigrum, Cinnamomum verum, Withania somnifera, and Acmella oleracea. If left untreated, numbness in hands can severely impact daily activities, causing difficulties in performing tasks, driving, and even leading to emotional distress, social isolation, and decreased productivity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with numbness in hands because of its unique blend of nutrients and antioxidants that promote healthy blood circulation. The capsules contain compounds that help to improve nerve function, reducing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, they are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and other essential fatty acids that nourish the skin and underlying tissues, allowing for greater sensitivity and sensation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair tree capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves, which can contribute to numbness or tingling sensations in the hands.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in maidenhair tree capsules help protect the nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can also contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Neuroprotective properties: Maidenhair tree capsules contain neuroprotective compounds that may help protect the nerve cells from damage and degeneration, potentially relieving numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: These terpenoid compounds help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which in turn can alleviate numbness in hands caused by poor blood flow.
Flavonoids (Bilobalide and Bilobetin): As powerful antioxidants, these flavonoids help protect blood vessels from oxidative damage, promote healthy blood flow, and reduce numbness in hands.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby improving blood circulation and alleviating numbness in hands.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help improve blood circulation and reduce numbness in hands.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain a high amount of ginkgolides, which can help improve blood flow to the extremities and reduce symptoms of numbness and tingling.
Barks: The barks are utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote blood circulation, alleviating numbness in hands.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer on a paper bag for 7-10 days to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with a binding agent like gelatin or starch in a 1:2 ratio.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and compress the mixture into capsules for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with numbness in hands because they contain a concentrated extract of Bacopa monnieri, an herb traditionally used to enhance cognitive function and improve memory. The active compounds in brahmi capsules have been shown to increase the production of neurotransmitters that stimulate nerve endings, reducing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect nerves from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and alleviating numbness and prickling sensations in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Brahmi capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the nerves, which can cause numbness in the hands, and alleviate associated pain.

Neuroprotective property: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds help protect the nerve cells from damage and degeneration, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, thereby promoting healthy nerve function.

Antioxidant property: Brahmi capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which can damage nerves and cause numbness, helping to maintain overall nerve health.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides, a type of saponin glycoside, may help with numbness in hands by improving blood circulation and nerve function, thereby reducing symptoms of numbness and tingling.

Bacosterol: Bacosterol, a triterpenoid saponin, may help alleviate numbness in hands by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to numbness.

Clerodane diterpenes: Clerodane diterpenes, a class of terpenoids, may help with numbness in hands by modulating the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, which can improve nerve function and reduce symptoms of numbness and tingling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their bioactive compounds, leaves are used to create extracts that may help alleviate numbness and other nerve-related issues.
Roots: The roots contain bacosides, which are responsible for the herb's medicinal properties, including potential relief from numbness and other neural problems.
Stems: The stems of Bacopa monnieri may also be used to create extracts that help with numbness and other nerve-related conditions due to their bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems from mature plants on a sunny morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20g of dried bacopa monnieri into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2g of the powder with 8g of vegetable cellulose or gelatin in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the mixture into empty gelatin or vegetable cellulose capsules using a manual encapsulator for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with numbness in hands because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin, present in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can cause numbness or tingling sensations in the hands. By reducing swelling and repairing damaged tissue, turmeric capsules help restore normal nerve function, alleviating symptoms of numbness, tingling, and pain. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to address hand numbness, promoting overall well-being and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves, which may be contributing to numbness in the hands.
Curcumin's antioxidant properties: Curcumin's antioxidant properties can help protect the nerves from oxidative damage and inflammation, which may be causing numbness in the hands.

Curcumin's neuroprotective effects: Turmeric's curcumin has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which means it can help promote nerve health and function, potentially alleviating symptoms of numbness in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound helps with numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuropathy.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, DMC helps to protect the nerves from damage and promote healing, which can help alleviate numbness in hands.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): This compound has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which can help to repair and protect damaged nerves, reducing numbness and other symptoms of neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are the primary part used due to their high concentration of curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Curcuma longa roots are also used as they contain a significant amount of curcumin, which helps to reduce pain and numbness in the hands.
Stems: Some sources indicate that the stems of Curcuma longa may also be used, although it's not as widely recognized for its pain-relieving properties as the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried curcuma longa root powder and 50g of gelatin powder.
Weigh out 1500 capsules 0.5ml each and 50g of gelatin powder.
Mix 50g of gelatin powder with 200ml of boiling water for 5 minutes stirring constantly.
Combine the gelatin mixture with 500g of dried curcuma longa root powder and mix for 10 minutes.
Fill the 1500 capsules with the mixture and seal using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with numbness in hands because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to improve blood flow and oxygenation to peripheral nerves. This increased circulation can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations caused by nerve damage or compression. Additionally, St John's wort's antioxidant properties may also help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve dysfunction. By promoting healthy nerve function and circulation, St John's wort capsules may provide relief from numbness in the hands.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This bioactive compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Hypotensive action: St. John's Wort capsules have been shown to improve blood circulation and lower blood pressure, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands caused by poor blood flow.

Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort is considered an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promote balance. This property may help reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms that can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that helps with numbness in hands by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and norepinephrine, which play a role in pain sensation and transmission.
N-Acetylserotonin: N-Acetylserotonin is a phenolic compound that helps with numbness in hands by increasing the levels of serotonin in the body, which can help reduce pain and inflammation.

Flavonoids (including rutin and quercetin): Flavonoids are a type of phenolic compound that help with numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected areas, which can help alleviate numbness and pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are used due to their high concentration of hypericin, a compound that may help alleviate numbness and pain.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain hypericin and other active compounds that may help reduce numbness and inflammation in the hands.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of flavonoids and hypericin, which may help alleviate numbness and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the early morning after the dew has dried off the plants.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 35-40°c for 1-2 hours using a dehydrator or a low-temperature oven.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered hypericum perforatum with a binding agent such as gelatin and water to create a uniform mixture for 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 300-500 milligrams of the hypericum perforatum mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with numbness in hands because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of numbness in the hands. The herbal extract also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the nervous system and promote circulation, thereby reducing tingling sensations and restoring sensation to the hands. Additionally, ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to balance cortisol levels, further contributing to a reduction in numbness and overall improved hand function.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic property helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of numbness in hands, by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the hands, which can cause numbness and tingling sensations by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the affected areas.

Antioxidant property: Ashwagandha's antioxidant property helps to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by neutralizing oxidative stress and promoting overall cellular health.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Withaferin A, a key alkaloid in Withania somnifera, has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and promoting nerve function.
Glycosides: Withanosides, a group of glycosides found in Withania somnifera, have antioxidant properties that can help protect nerve cells from damage, potentially alleviating numbness in hands.

Triterpenoids: Withanolides, a type of triterpenoid in Withania somnifera, have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help reduce numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and promoting nerve regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are rich in bioactive compounds that help to relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used to make capsules for numbness in hands due to their high content of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Withania somnifera are used to make capsules for numbness in hands due to their high content of withanolides and other bioactive compounds that help to relieve pain and numbness.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried withania somnifera root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground withania somnifera powder with 20g of starch and 5g of silica gel for uniformity.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the withania somnifera mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Seal the capsules using a manual capsule sealer for 2 minutes to prevent powder leakage.
Label the capsules with the name withania somnifera, dosage, and preparation date using a permanent marker.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerves. The herb has been traditionally used to relieve pain and discomfort associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, including numbness and tingling sensations in the fingers. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, devil's claw capsules may help restore normal nerve function and alleviate numbness in the hands, allowing for improved sensation and mobility.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Harpagophytum procumbens contribute to its pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Devil's claw have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate numbness in the hands by promoting healthy nerve function.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of harpagoside, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: Like roots, rhyzomes are also used for their harpagoside content, which may help alleviate numbness and pain in hands.
Barks: The barks of Harpagophytum procumbens contain harpagoside and other compounds that may contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of harpagophytum procumbens roots in the winter season when the plant is dormant.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 250 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 1 gram of starch or cellulose powder to act as a filler.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with numbness in hands because they contain a natural ingredient that has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce numbness and tingling sensations. The herb valerian root is known for its ability to calm the nerves and promote relaxation, making it an effective treatment for conditions such as peripheral neuropathy, which can cause numbness in the hands. remedy_properties: Sedative Properties: Valerian root's sedative properties can help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant compounds present in valerian capsules may help to protect nerves from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and weakness in the hands.

Nerve Stimulating Properties: Valerian root has been shown to stimulate nerve function and increase blood flow to the extremities, which can help to relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: A sesquiterpene acid that helps relax the nervous system, reducing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by promoting a calming effect on the nerves.
Isovaleric acid: A terpene that acts as a natural muscle relaxant, reducing muscle spasms and numbness in the hands by relaxing the muscles and improving blood flow.

Valerenol: A sesquiterpene that has a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, which is responsible for the sedative and numbing effects.
Leaves: They contain volatile oils and flavonoids that contribute to the numbing sensation and relaxation properties.
Roots (again, as they are the most used part): Roots are also rich in valerianone and valerenal, which help in relieving numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root and store it in an airtight container for later use.
Dry the harvested root in a low-temperature oven at 105-110 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250-500 milligrams of the powder with a suitable capsule filler such as gelatin or vegetarian cellulose.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with numbness in hands because they improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. Glycosides: Specifically, the glycoside content in Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides, may help improve blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the hands, which can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of panax ginseng roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the panax ginseng roots in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Grind 30g of the dried panax ginseng roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powdered panax ginseng root each.
Store the herbal panax ginseng capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid compound present in hawthorn that acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain associated with numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in hawthorn capsules has vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow to the extremities, including the hands, potentially alleviating numbness caused by reduced circulation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Crataegus monogyna capsules, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help alleviate numbness in hands by their antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Crataegus monogyna, such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, help with numbness in hands by their ability to reduce inflammation and improve vascular function, thereby promoting better circulation to the hands.

remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make capsules for numbness in hands due to their potential anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for numbness in hands due to their potential to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.
Fruits: Used to make capsules for numbness in hands due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce numbness and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried berries from crataegus monogyna trees that are at least 5 years old.
Dry the harvested berries in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 8 hours.
Grind 200g of the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100g of gelatin and 20g of glycerin to form a uniform mixture.
Fill 100 capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

The active compounds in maypop, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to increase the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin and GABA, which help regulate nerve function and relieve numbness and tingling sensations. By promoting healthy nerve transmission, maypop capsules can effectively alleviate hand numbness and restore normal sensation in individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maypop capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation, which can alleviate numbness in hands caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or peripheral neuropathy. This property helps to decrease swelling and pressure on nerves, relieving numbness and tingling sensations.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in Maypop capsules help protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to numbness in hands caused by oxidative stress. By neutralizing free radicals, these antioxidants may reduce inflammation and promote healthy nerve function.

Nerve-protecting flavonoids: Maypop capsules contain flavonoids that have been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which can help preserve the integrity of nerve cells and fibers. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which may alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Harmane alkaloid: This alkaloid has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce numbness and discomfort in the hands.

Leaves: Utilized because they contain flavonoids and alkaloids, which help to reduce pain and numbness.
Barks: Employed for its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate numbness and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the morning when they are dry.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground material with 100 grams of gelatin and 100 milliliters of distilled water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them for storage and consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The capillary-rich properties of yarrow help to stimulate blood flow, which can alleviate numbness caused by poor circulation. Additionally, yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and pressure on nerves, allowing them to function properly and relieving numbness. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, measuring out 2 cups for this recipe.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by rinsing it gently under cold running water and draining thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned achillea millefolium with 4 cups of pure filtered water in a large pot, bringing it to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to steep and release their active compounds.
Strain the liquid from the plant material using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, then fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with numbness in hands because they promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and improve blood circulation. The calming properties of lavender help to alleviate nerve compression and tension, which can cause numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender may help to reduce swelling and irritation that can contribute to numbness. remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to numbness in the hands by alleviating pressure on nerve endings.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in joints and tissues, which can help relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Analgesic and Anxiolytic Properties: Lavender's analgesic (pain-relieving) and anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help to calm nervous system activity, which may contribute to numbness in the hands by reducing stress and anxiety-related tension in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate numbness in hands.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered lavender with 500 milliliters of vodka in a glass jar, allowing it to steep for six hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids, and store the resulting tincture in an airtight container at room temperature.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-half teaspoon of the prepared lavender tincture, sealing them tightly to create 90 capsules for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with numbness in hands because they promote healthy circulation and reduce inflammation. By inhibiting the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), saw palmetto may help alleviate compression on the nerves, which can cause numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hands. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the joints and soft tissues, allowing for improved nerve function and sensation. As a result, many individuals have reported relief from hand numbness and improved overall mobility after taking saw palmetto supplements regularly.
remedy_properties: Inhibitory effects on 5-alpha-reductase: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit this enzyme helps reduce the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to nerve compression and numbness in hands.

Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can damage nerves and contribute to numbness, tingling, and other symptoms associated with conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and fatty acids, may help reduce swelling and compression of the nerves in the hands, which can alleviate numbness and improve nerve function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in fatty acids, particularly oleic and linoleic acid, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, alleviating numbness in hands.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant contain flavonoids and phytosterols that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve numbness in hands by reducing swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried serenoa repens root powder.
Mix the powder with 50 grams of rice flour and 10 grams of gum arabic.
Add 20 drops of vodka and 5 ml of glycerin to the mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with a mixture of the herbal blend and lubricants.
Store the capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitis vinifera leaves measuring exactly 20 grams for each capsule filling.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for eight hours.
Grind the dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within two minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered herb with one tablespoon of organic beeswax and five drops of lavender essential oil.
Fill 100-count capsules with the herbal mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 500 grams of high-quality green tea leaves, specifically camellia sinensis varietal.
Grind the green tea leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of the ground green tea powder with 100 grams of dried ginger root powder in a bowl for 3 minutes.
Add 50 grams of ginkgo biloba leaf powder to the mixture and stir well for an additional 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems from well-maintained plants with optimal growing conditions.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and ensure quality.
Grind 30 grams of dried capsicum annuum leaves and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 500 milligrams of ground capsicum annuum powder with 1000 milligrams of gelatin capsules and fill them immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year to maintain potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers, ensuring at least 2 ounces of plant material.
Dry the plant material in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days, or until brittle and crumbly.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Mix 250 milligrams of powdered verbascum thapsus with 500 milligrams of gelatin in 1 tablespoon of water, stirring for 10 minutes.
Fill capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, allowing 30 days between each capsule preparation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots extract taraxacum officinale by washing them gently with water and removing any debris.
Dry the extracted roots using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 20 grams of dried dandelion root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to create a uniform consistency.
Mix the powdered dandelion root with 10 milliliters of glycerin and 50 milliliters of water to create a smooth paste preparation time is 30 minutes.
Fill empty #00 gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the prepared dandelion root paste and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried red clover flowers trifolium pratense and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the ground red clover powder with 10 grams of silica gel as an anti-caking agent.
Mix 500 mg of the combined herbal blend with 250 mg of organic rice flour to fill capsules.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture and store in an airtight container for up to six months.
Consume three capsules twice daily after meals for 30 days to alleviate numbness in hands.
